title: The SEO Signal - Issue 1
subject: AI search just got its own analytics — and most SEOs aren't watching
preheader: Moz's AI Research Toolkit, what GEO means, and 3 things to check this week
bodyHtml:
The SEO Signal

Clarity Engine AI · Issue 1 · April 16, 2026

Welcome to the first issue of The SEO Signal — your weekly briefing on what’s changing at the intersection of SEO and AI. If search is evolving, you should be too. Let’s get into it.

Moz Launches AI Research Toolkit

Moz just released a dedicated AI Research Toolkit designed to help SEOs understand how their content performs inside AI-generated answers. The toolkit tracks AI citation frequency, surfaces which queries trigger AI overviews, and benchmarks your visibility against competitors in generative search results. This is the first major toolset purpose-built for Generative Engine Optimization — and it signals that GEO is no longer a fringe concept.

Read our full breakdown  |  Try Moz free

What Is GEO (Generative Engine Optimization)?

GEO is the practice of optimizing your content so that AI search engines — like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews — cite, reference, or surface it in their generated responses. Unlike traditional SEO, which targets link placement on a results page, GEO focuses on making your content structured, authoritative, and citation-friendly so that large language models choose it as a source. Think of it as the next layer of search visibility: you’re not just ranking — you’re being quoted.

Deep dive: GEO and why it matters now

3 Things to Check This Week

1. Search your brand in ChatGPT and Perplexity. What comes up? Is the information accurate? Are competitors mentioned instead of you? This is your GEO baseline.

2. Check if your competitors appear in AI answers for your target keywords. Search your top 5 keywords in Perplexity and Google AI Overviews. Note who gets cited and what content format they use.

3. Review your content’s structure — is it citation-friendly? AI models favor clear headings, concise definitions, structured data (FAQ schema, how-to markup), and authoritative sourcing. If your pages are walls of text, they’re getting skipped.

Tool Spotlight

SE Ranking

While GEO is the future, traditional SEO metrics still matter today. SE Ranking gives you rank tracking, site audits, backlink monitoring, and competitive analysis in one dashboard — at a price point that won’t break a solo operator’s budget. Pair it with Moz’s AI toolkit and you’ve got both sides of the search equation covered.

Try SE Ranking →

That’s it for Issue 1. Reply to this email if you’ve got a question, a tool worth reviewing, or a hot take on GEO — we read every reply.
